channel.title: +Articles on Vishvas's notes
channel.description: Recent content in +Articles on Vishvas's notes

item.title: China youth control 2021
item.description:
The Triumph and Terror of Wang Huning – Palladium
N.S. Lyons is an analyst and writer living and working in Washington, D.C. He is the author of The Upheaval.

One day in August 2021, Zhao Wei disappeared. For one of China’s best-known actresses to physically vanish from public view would have been enough to cause a stir on its own. But Zhao’s disappearing act was far more thorough: overnight, she was erased from the internet. Her Weibo social media page, with its 86 million followers, went offline, as did fan sites dedicated to her.+++(5)+++ Searches for her many films and television shows returned no results on streaming sites. Zhao’s name was scrubbed from the credits of projects she had appeared in or directed, replaced with a blank space. Online discussions uttering her name were censored. Suddenly, little trace remained that the 45-year-old celebrity had ever existed.

item.title: Fundamental Principles of a State’s Well-being
item.description:
Author - DVG

Intro

[[Fundamental Principles of a State’s Well-being - Part 1 Source: prekshaa]]

विस्तारः (द्रष्टुं नोद्यम्)

This is the final part of the ten-part English translation of the epilogue of D V Gundappa’s Jnapakachitrashaale – Vol. 4 – Mysurina Dewanaru. Edited by Hari Ravikumar and Raghavendra G S.

rāṣṭraṃ vā aśvamedhaḥ…
– Yajurveda (Taittirīya-brāhmaṇa 3.8.9)
kalpate ha vai tatra prajābhyo yogakṣemaḥ
yatraitena yajñena yajante
– Yajurveda (Taittirīya-brāhmaṇa 3.8.13)
The nation itself is the Aśvamedha (aśva represents the State)… with that both gain (yoga) and well-being (kṣema) of the citizens are achieved…
